justification: All 7 required rules identified (plus an extra BR-5 for manager approval). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), negative balance (true), 3-day advance (false), medical retroactive (false), rejected leave balance (true), contract end date (true), carryover cap (false). Multiple ambiguities surfaced in low_confidence_areas (calendar vs working days for 5-day window, definition of 'month worked', when balance is deducted). 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering accrual, negative balance, medical leave exception, carryover, and more. Confidence level of 82 is well-calibrated.

observations.text: All 7 required business rules identified with correct invariant classification — no misclassifications
Clear distinction between structural invariants (balance integrity, contract boundary) and configurable policies (accrual rate, carryover cap, notice period)
12 acceptance criteria far exceeding the 4 minimum, covering accrual, negative balance, medical leave exception, carryover, rejection, and contract boundary
Both key ambiguities surfaced: 5-day retroactive window calendar vs working days, and contract-end mid-period implications (via edge cases)
Rich edge cases section covering mid-process accrual, pending requests at year-end, and contract end date changes
Bonus BR-5 (manager approval authority) correctly classified as invariant: false
Bounded context identified as single 'Leave Management' rather than distinguishing 'leave lifecycle' and 'leave balance management' as separate bounded contexts
Escalations array is empty — the ambiguities were surfaced in low_confidence_areas but not formally escalated
